page: OVHcloud b3-32 is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz, configured with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. The hardware profile includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a local 200 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test places this server in the top tier (top 10%), supported by strong single-core Geekbench scores and bzip3 single-threaded decompression. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average range. Memory performance is notable, with a strong Passmark memory score and strong large-block write bandwidth of 96.92 GB/sec, though read bandwidth and database operations remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the b3-32 offers a balanced resource density, combining dedicated AMD EPYC cores with high-speed local NVMe storage without the premium of specialized compute or memory-heavy instances. This makes it highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, text processing, and light database operations. The lack of hyperthreading ensures predictable per-core performance, though the absence of a GPU excludes it from accelerated parallel computing tasks.
description: OVHcloud b3-32 is a general-purpose virtual server powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, offering a non-hyperthreaded architecture with 32.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 200 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory write bandwidth, while multi-core operations and database performance align with average mid-range standards. This resource configuration provides a balanced, cost-effective profile for general-purpose applications, web serving, and text processing workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and fast local storage.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-32 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 200 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.

tagline: A balanced virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, high-speed NVMe storage, and strong single-core performance.
